key_insight: Chat-ISV turns scattered steel-industry VOC knowledge into a traceable knowledge graph so LLMs can answer specialized pollution-control questions with less hallucination.
engineering_relevance: The framework supports reliable decision support for industrial emissions management by linking process, pollutant, and control-technology evidence in a queryable environment.
method: Parsed a curated VOC literature corpus and built a Neo4j knowledge graph with 27,180 nodes and 81,779 semantic edges.
Used prompt-constrained extraction, chunk-centered topology optimization, and multi-agent routing for retrieval and answer generation.
Added source-backtracking, local literature retrieval, open-domain knowledge access, and interactive subgraph visualization.
evidence_signal: Benchmark tests and 400 expert blind evaluations reported 96.93% precision, 72.63% recall, F1 = 0.830, and a mean score of 1.69/2.00; topology optimization reduced isolated nodes from 57% to 4.08%.
watch_item: Performance depends on high-quality curated literature and graph construction, so coverage gaps or extraction errors could still affect answer reliability.
paper_title: Traceable Knowledge Graph Reasoning Enables LLM-Assisted Decision Support for Industrial VOCs in the Steel Industry
